input.instruction: Assess whether the evidence package has the gap this pattern describes. If the package is complete for this COU, the correct disposition is to accept — do NOT over-act on an alarming-looking but adequately-addressed signal. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge.

fired_pattern.definition: Surrogate is an older version; re-validation status for the COU is recorded.

case_context.cou: the older model version (v2) informs a flow decision

answer_key.gold_mechanism: The older version was explicitly re-validated for this COU (3% on a current reference) and the newer-version changes are documented irrelevant to this regime; version age without a COU-relevant change is not a defeater.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.justification: older version re-validated for this exact COU; newer changes documented irrelevant to the regime

forbidden_claims: restricts/rejects on version age despite recorded COU re-validation (over-action)
asserts the older version is as good as the newest in general (a verdict beyond the COU)

hardness.obvious_signal: the surrogate is an older model version (v2, current is v4) — looks stale
hardness.conflicting_signal: the older v2 was explicitly RE-validated for this exact COU (3% error on a current reference), and the v3/v4 changes are documented as features irrelevant to this COU's regime — version age without a relevant change is not a defeater

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 provenance: credibility attaches to the re-validated artifact for the COU, not to version recency; over-acting on age despite re-validation is over-action.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
